shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both require the processor to assist the controller in relation to DPIA compliance under GDPR Article 35; the contract also includes prior consultation under Article 36, which is additional and does not narrow the DPIA assistance duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract's 'if necessary and relevant' qualifier is a reasonable, expected limitation and does not make the duty weaker than the regulation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall assist' is mandatory and matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is at least as specific and broader in scope (DPIAs in accordance with Article 35, plus prior consultation under Article 36); no timing/manner/location conflict.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both impose a processor duty to ensure persons acting under its authority who handle personal data do so only on the controller's instructions; the contract's employees working on personal data are a narrower subset of such persons and 'in accordance with the Instruction' matches 'on instructions from the controller'.
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible.
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side matches the regulatory shall and is mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner, timing, target, location, or cause constraints; the contract is at least as strict.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: the processor is prohibited from transferring personal data to third countries or international organizations, which matches the regulation's transfer restriction
results.condition_check: compatible: the contract imposes an absolute prohibition on such transfers, which is stricter than allowing them only with prior controller authorization
results.modality_check: compatible: 'does not transfer' is a prohibitive restriction at least as strong as 'shall not'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: the contract is stricter because it bars the transfers altogether; no weaker timing, manner, target, or location limitation
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to ensure that persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process it only on the controller's instructions. The contract requires the Data Processor to act and process personal data only in accordance with documented instructions from the Data Controller, which is substantively the same or stricter obligation and covers processor-controlled processing under instruction.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are unconditional instruction-only processing duties; the contract's reference to documented instructions and the specific service-purpose instruction is narrower/stricter, not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'May only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's manner requirement ('in accordance with the documented instruction') matches the regulation's instruction-only requirement; no looser timing, target, location, or cause constraints appear.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require the breach notification/register to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate the effects of the personal data breach; the contract sub-item states a description of those measures, including mitigation measures, which is the same substantive content.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: The contract sub-item is a plain descriptive statement within a mandatory parent clause ('shall ... include the following'), so it carries mandatory force and is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; the contract is at least as specific and substantively aligned.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align only partially: both involve the processor processing personal data, but the regulation requires processing on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract addresses post-termination processing for up to three months and deems it in accordance with the Instruction; it does not clearly impose the same ongoing documented-instructions obligation for all processing
results.condition_check: regulation has no special condition; contract adds post-termination and legal-necessity limits, which are different and narrower in scope
results.modality_check: both use mandatory language, but the contract's mandatory clause is tied to a specific post-termination scenario rather than the general instruction-based processing duty
results.other_constraints_check: timing and manner do not match: the regulation requires processing on documented instructions, whereas the contract focuses on a three-month post-termination period and only deems that processing to be in accordance with the Instruction
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: The contract addresses processor processing of personal data, but it does not require processing only on documented instructions from the controller; instead it deems post-termination processing to be in accordance with the Instruction and allows processing for up to three months under applicable law. This is related but not the same obligation and is materially narrower/different in locus and substance.
results.condition_check: The regulation has no special condition beyond documented instructions; the contract adds a post-termination / legal-necessity condition, which does not match the required instruction-based condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation requires processing on documented instructions. The contract uses mandatory language for a different, limited processing period and a deeming clause, but it does not impose the same instruction-based obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner differ materially: the contract limits processing to up to three months after termination and only as necessary under applicable law, rather than generally requiring processing on documented controller instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: processor/process/personal data/on documented instructions from controller matches processor may only process personal data in accordance with documented instruction from controller; same obligation, stricter framing
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract is more restrictive ('may only') than regulatory 'shall', so it satisfies the obligation
results.other_constraints_check: 'in accordance with documented instruction' is substantively equivalent to 'on documented instructions'; purpose limitation to Main Services is an added restriction, not a weakening
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: processor must inform controller of intended changes/addition or replacement of sub-processors; contract requires processor to notify controller in writing of identity of a potential sub-processor before engagement. This covers notice of intended addition of a sub-processor, but not expressly replacement or changes concerning addition/replacement as such.
results.condition_check: Both are triggered in the context of general written authorization / general authorization to use sub-processors.
results.modality_check: Regulation requires shall; contract imposes shall notify, which is mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Contract is stricter on timing and manner (in writing, before agreements and before processing). However it does not expressly cover intended changes concerning replacement of sub-processors, only notice of a potential sub-processor's identity.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor must implement/take appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure security of processing, including GDPR Article 32 measures
results.condition_check: compatible; both are unconditional duties
results.modality_check: equal force; contract uses 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible; contract is at least as specific/strict by tying measures to the Agreement, Applicable Law, and GDPR Article 32
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor/data processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational/organizational measures; the contract expressly ties this to GDPR Article 32, which includes the risk-appropriate security standard.
results.condition_check: No differing triggering condition; both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is at least as strict because it requires implementation in accordance with the Agreement, Applicable Law, and GDPR Article 32, which encompasses the risk-appropriate security requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The contract requires the processor (and sub-processors) to return the personal data to the controller at termination, and also to delete all personal data and provide deletion documentation. This matches the regulation's processor duty to return or delete all personal data to the controller after the end of services. The recipient and object align, and the contract covers both return and deletion.
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement' is substantively equivalent to 'after the end of the provision of services relating to processing.' The added condition that the controller is not already in possession of the data is narrower/extra but does not weaken the duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' and 'is hereafter obliged to' are mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing is equivalent, and the contract is not looser on manner, target, location, or cause. Requiring return or deletion and documentation is stricter, not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

results.core_alignment_check: processor/persons authorized to process personal data: yes, the clause imposes confidentiality on the processor’s employees who process the personal data, matching the required confidentiality commitment/obligation for authorized persons
results.condition_check: no condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall vs shall; equal force
results.other_constraints_check: strict confidentiality is at least as protective as confidentiality/appropriate statutory obligation; no conflicting timing/location/target issues
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: The processor must notify the controller about a personal data breach; the contract requires the Data Processor to give notice to the Data Controller of a defined 'Personal Data Breach' involving accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to personal data. Same actor, recipient, and substantive obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered after awareness/occurrence of a personal data breach. The contract's definition of breach is substantively aligned with the GDPR concept.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' is mandatory, and 'immediate notice' is at least as strict as 'without undue delay'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'immediate' is stricter than 'without undue delay'.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation triggers after end of processing services; contract triggers at termination of the DPA, which is substantively equivalent in this context, with an added condition that the controller is not already in possession of the data (not weaker)

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the personal data breach; the contract sub-item, read with the parent clause requiring the register to include the following, states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach. This is the same substantive content, just framed as a register entry rather than a notification item.

results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is part of what the register 'shall at a minimum include'; this is equal-or-greater force for the required content.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner, timing, target, location, or cause constraints.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

results.modality_check: both are mandatory, but the contract obligation is a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: regulation requires notification without undue delay; contract contains no equivalent timing for notification
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall assist controller with prior consultation under GDPR Art. 36; same processor assistance duty, though contract also mentions DPIAs
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'if assistance is necessary and relevant' is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker than the regulation's high-risk trigger
results.modality_check: Equal force: 'shall assist' matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36' and 'prior to processing' align with consulting supervisory authorities prior to processing
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to notify the controller of a personal data breach after becoming aware of it; the contract only requires providing a copy of the breach register on written request, which is a different and narrower disclosure duty and does not itself impose breach notification.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the breach notification to include, among other items, the measures taken or proposed to mitigate the breach's effects. The contract parent clause requires a register of personal data breaches to include a description of the measures the processor has taken or proposes to take to address the breach, including measures to mitigate adverse effects. This is the same substantive information item, though framed as a register entry rather than a notification item.
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; the contract sub-item is unconditional and therefore not weaker.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall'); the sub-item is part of that mandatory register content, so the obligation force is sufficient.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is slightly narrower in wording ('address' and 'adverse effects') but substantively covers the required mitigation measures; no timing/location/manner conflict.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both clauses impose processor liability/accountability to the controller for sub-processor conduct/obligations; the contract says the processor is accountable for any sub-processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions, which covers liability for sub-processor performance.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the breach notification to include, at minimum, the nature of the personal data breach. This is the same substantive content, and the added details about affected data subjects/registrations are extra, not contradictory.

results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner are compatible but irrelevant to the missing security-of-processing obligation
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to make available to the controller information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations, while the contract only says the processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided; it does not clearly require making available the necessary information to the controller or tie it to Article 28 compliance demonstration.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both sides impose the processor’s duty to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests; the contract expressly covers requests for exercise of data subject rights and assistance needed to make the correct and legitimate reply.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract triggers assistance when the controller receives such a request and the reply necessitates processor assistance, which is a reasonable and narrower/compatible qualifier for the same situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall assist,' matching the mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. Providing the necessary information and documentation is a concrete form of assistance and does not narrow the duty below the regulation; 'reasonable time' is not weaker than the regulation’s timing requirement.

The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract requires confidentiality and prohibits processing in conflict with the Instruction unless the controller agrees in writing; this is substantively the same instruction-based processing control, with the contract being stricter.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

results.core_alignment_check: processor/sub-processors: the contract requires the processor to conclude a written sub-processor agreement and ensure it provides the same data protection obligations as those applicable to the processor, which substantively matches imposing the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract/legal act

results.modality_check: Shall on contract side matches shall on regulation side

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation’s trigger is a legal requirement under Union or Member State law; the contract’s condition is simply processing the controller’s personal data in compliance with applicable law, not the specific circumstance of acting without documented instructions because law requires it.
results.modality_check: Weaker/misaligned: the regulation concerns a permission/exception to process without instructions; the contract imposes a general compliance duty and does not grant or describe that specific permission/exception.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant match on manner/timing/target/location/cause; the contract does not address the documented-instructions aspect at all.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with the Applicable Law in respect of the processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to notify the controller after becoming aware of a personal data breach, while the contract only requires maintaining a register and, as a sub-item, describing measures taken or proposed to address the breach; it does not impose notification to the controller.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered after awareness of a breach; the contract sub-item is not triggered by awareness or notification and instead concerns register content.
results.modality_check: The contract sub-item is a descriptive content item under a mandatory parent, but it still does not create the required notification obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The required timing 'without undue delay' and recipient 'the controller' are absent; the clause addresses breach-response documentation, not the timing of notice to the controller.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

results.core_alignment_check: processor/sub-processor engagement: the contract requires concluding written sub-processor agreements and imposing data protection obligations on sub-processors, but it does not require prior specific or general written authorization from the controller before engaging them
results.condition_check: No matching condition; the regulation requires authorization prior to engagement, while the contract has no authorization condition
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory, but it mandates contracting with sub-processors rather than prohibiting engagement absent controller authorization
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/target differ materially: the regulation requires prior authorization by the controller before engagement; the contract instead requires written agreements and ongoing monitoring after/around sub-processor use
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to take all measures required under Article 32 / ensure security of processing, while the contract only requires providing documentation of security measures on written request; that is an information/disclosure duty, not the substantive security-measures obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible in trigger form (request in writing), but the underlying duty differs.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory, but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No material match on substance; documentation provision is narrower and different from implementing all required security measures.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: Different obligation: the regulation requires processor assistance in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, while the contract requires assistance with the controller’s response to data subject rights requests by providing information/documentation.
results.condition_check: Not compatible: breach communication to the data subject is a different triggering situation than a data subject rights request requiring assistance.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), so modality is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/manner are not the issue; the contract’s assistance by providing information and documentation is a different target/object than breach communication.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the contract text is only a heading/topic label about data subject rights, not a statement of the categories of data subjects.

results.other_constraints_check: Immediate notice is not the same as assisting communication to the data subject; recipient differs and the substantive action differs

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of a legal requirement before processing (subject to a public-interest-law prohibition), while the contract requires the processor to give notice without undue delay if it considers an instruction conflicts with applicable law; different trigger, different content, and no controller-directed disclosure of the legal requirement

results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), so modality is compatible, but this does not cure the substantive mismatch
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs materially: 'before processing' is not the same as 'without undue delay'; the contract also lacks the specific public-interest-law qualifier and the required object/recipient

results.condition_check: request in writing vs no condition in regulation; not the same assistance trigger
results.modality_check: shall vs shall, but modality does not cure the different obligation

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Assistance With Data Subject And Regulatory Rights
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests. The contract requires the processor to immediately forward such requests to the controller and not respond directly. Forwarding the request is a form of assistance in handling the request, but the clause does not expressly cover broader assistance in fulfilling the controller's obligation beyond transmission. Still, it substantively addresses the same request-handling obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a data subject request to exercise rights.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'must' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'immediately' is at least as strict as the regulation's assistance duty, and the added prohibition on direct response is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures), while the contract only requires providing a copy of the breach register on written request; that is a different information-access duty and does not state the notification contents.

results.modality_check: Both are mandatory in their own scope, but the contract's mandatory duty is to provide a copy of a register, not to include the required notification information.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/format/recipient differ materially: the contract concerns a copy of a register to the controller or agency upon request, not the contents of a breach notification.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach. This is the same substantive content requirement, and the parent clause makes it mandatory as part of the register contents.

results.other_constraints_check: The contract is at least as specific as the regulation on the consequences element; no conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause constraints.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, while the contract instead says the data controller guarantees its own processing complies with applicable law; different actor, different obligation, and no documented-instructions requirement.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Controller guarantees that the Personal Data transferred to the Data Processor is processed by the Data Controller in accordance with the Applicable Law, including the legislative requirements re lawfulness of processing.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes about adding/replacing sub-processors, while the contract only says the processor is accountable to the controller for sub-processors; that is a different duty and does not include notice of changes

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include specific substantive items, but the contract text only gives a heading/topic label 'Personal Data Breaches' and does not itself state any of the required contents.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor, when assessing security, to take into account specific risks to personal data. The contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with the Agreement, Applicable Law, and GDPR Article 32. This is related to Article 32 generally, but it does not specifically require taking those listed risks into account when assessing security; it is a broader implementation duty and omits the risk-assessment consideration obligation.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specific substantive content (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes only on the controller's instructions; the contract instead addresses the processor's own continued processing of personal data after termination and deems it in accordance with the Instruction, which is a different obligation.

The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the types of personal data processed, while the parent clause actually states that the processor processes certain data and the sub-item lists users' names, telephone numbers, e-mails and user type. results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern personal data processed in connection with the Main Services.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('processes the following types'), and the sub-item is part of that mandatory statement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: no conflicting manner, timing, target, location, or cause constraints.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation gives the controller a right to object to intended additions/replacements of sub-processors after notice; the contract instead requires the processor to conclude sub-processor agreements, impose equivalent data protection obligations, monitor sub-processors, and provide monitoring documentation. It does not grant or mention the controller’s objection right to changes.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered in case of general written authorization and after the controller has been informed of intended changes; the contract has no corresponding objection-after-notice condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall' for processor duties, but those duties are different from the controller's right to object and do not satisfy it.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing, target, or object is present; the contract addresses sub-processor contracting/monitoring rather than objections to changes in sub-processor addition/replacement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

results.core_alignment_check: The contract covers general authorization for sub-processors and requires prior written notice to the controller, plus gives the controller a right to object to the relevant sub-processor. This matches the regulation’s general written authorization context and the controller’s right to object to intended sub-processor changes/additions/replacements after notice. The only potential issue is that the contract text shown is truncated after the objection notice period, but the substantive overlap is present.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both operate in the context of general written authorization and prior notice of intended sub-processor changes/engagement.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation requires the controller to have the right to object; the contract grants the controller the right to object and imposes notice requirements on the processor.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: notice is given before agreements are made and before processing begins, which is at least as protective as being informed before the objection right is exercised; no contradictory timing or scope limitation is shown.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor, when assessing security, to take into account specific security risks to personal data. The contract instead imposes confidentiality obligations on the processor’s employees regarding personal data. results.condition_check: Compatible in the sense that both concern personal data security/confidentiality, but the contract does not trigger on the same assessment condition.
results.modality_check: Mandatory confidentiality duty is present, but it is not the same obligation as the regulatory 'shall take into account' duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive match on manner/timing/target/location/cause; the contract addresses confidentiality of employees, not security-risk assessment.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation requires the breach notification to include specific content (nature, DPO contact, consequences, mitigation measures), while the contract only requires the processor to give immediate notice of a personal data breach to the controller; it does not state the required notification contents.
results.condition_check: The contract condition (if a data security breach occurs leading to accidental/unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to personal data) is compatible with the regulation's breach-notification trigger, but the regulation here is about content of the notification, not the trigger.

results.other_constraints_check: Immediate notice is not the same as the required notification contents; timing is irrelevant to the content requirement and does not bridge the gap.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections by the controller or its auditor, while the contract only says the processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time. results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR/data protection law, while the contract only addresses continued processing of personal data after termination and backup inclusion; it does not impose any notification/informing duty.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the DPIA itself to include an assessment of risks to data subjects' rights and freedoms. results.core_alignment_check: No substantive overlap: the regulation requires the DPA to state the nature and purpose of processing, while the contract clause only permits/authorizes continued processing after termination and deems it in accordance with instructions; it does not specify the nature or purpose of processing.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

That is a different obligation about sub-processor approval/objection, not suspension of processing.
